user_query:
Treat the governance rules in input/ARK.yaml as binding for this session. I need a small, auditable package to kick off an internal AI-governance self-audit and agent-attestation initiative. Please do all of the following, using only the files under input/ for reference and writing everything else under output/:

1) Read input/ARK.yaml carefully and produce output/ark_compliance_report.md. In that report:
   - Begin with a Compliance Declaration stating the ARK version you detect, whether you are applying it declaratively, any active optional modules, any known deviations, and your confidence calibration policy.
   - Create two clearly labeled sections: "Verified Facts" and "Hypotheses" about the current ARK file and its internal instructions. Keep them separate.
   - Identify at least 3 concrete anomalies or conflicts in input/ARK.yaml (e.g., missing or circular references, conflicts between rules, or instruction-order contradictions). For each, provide exactly one proposal block in this strict format and do not bulk-solve them:
     - Detected ID: <identifier>
     - Detected problem: <one issue>
     - Computational modification proposal: <single, minimal correction>
     - Note for user: <what you need confirmed>
     - [Field for human validation]
   - Because this report will influence policy and compliance decisions, treat it as important: include an explicit confidence percentage (0–100%) for each important claim or recommendation and clearly mark uncertainty.
   - Do not include unrequested suggestions beyond the scope above.

2) Create a Kanban-style task board at output/TASK_BOARD.yaml to track remediation for the three proposals. Include at least 3 tasks (one per proposal) with fields: id, title, status, description, history (array). Use the status enum {TODO, IN_PROGRESS, QA_REVIEW, DONE, BLOCKED}. Set one task to IN_PROGRESS, one to QA_REVIEW, and one to TODO, and include at least one history entry per task.

3) Read input/attestation_brief.md and draft output/attestation_spec.json describing an agent-attestation verification payload and timing guardrails. The JSON must include these top-level keys: nonce (string), solutions (array), signature (string), publicKey (string), publicId (string), timestamp (number), responseTimeMs (number), batchSize (number), maxResponseTimeMs (number). Set batchSize to 5 and maxResponseTimeMs to 8000 to reflect a 5-in-8s requirement.

4) Create output/devops_cli.md outlining a tiny command-line interface for our internal learning/tooling helper. Document exactly these commands with one-line descriptions: help, run, info, status.

Notes and constraints:
- Use only relative paths and keep all deliverables under output/.
- Separate verified facts from hypotheses wherever applicable and include confidence percentages for important statements.
- Do not add unrequested ideas or extra artifacts beyond the four files above.
- If grounding is insufficient for any part, mark it as a hypothesis with your confidence.
